title: Clionidae v0.1.5 Visual Library Styling Checkpoint
content:
Clionidae stayed on the same functional version this pass, but the design workspace was simplified into a more useful starter reference for future sites.

What changed:

  • Visual library reduced to essentials: the design page now keeps the structural specimen pieces without overcommitting to one finished brand direction, making it easier to borrow layouts and composition patterns for new projects.
  • Banner and typography calibration: top and bottom banners were aligned with the lighter Stallioneer-style frame, while multiple editorial type roles were tested so the page reads more clearly as a template surface.
  • Neutralized styling for reuse: color fills, extra borders, and block padding were stripped back so spacing, hierarchy, and content regions are easier to study when planning a new site.

This leaves Clionidae in a good place as a working reference: enough infrastructure to model real pages, but simplified enough to pull apart and reuse deliberately.
